title: Dock Badge Notifications
subtitle: Add live badge counts to dock icons
description:
# Idea: Dock Badge Notifications

## Summary
Surface the `badgeCounts` already tracked in AppContext as visual badge dots on dock module icons. When a cron job errors or a new agent event fires, a red dot appears on the relevant dock icon.

## Implementation Plan
1. Add a badge overlay to each dock button in `components/dock/dock.tsx`
2. Pull `badgeCounts` from `useApp()`
3. Render a small animated dot when count > 0
4. Clear on module focus via `clearModuleNotifications()`

## Estimated Effort
Small — 1–2 hour implementation.

## Why It Matters
Passive awareness without interruption. You see something needs attention without breaking flow.

title: Agent Activity Timeline
subtitle: Visual timeline of agent actions per session
description:
# Idea: Agent Activity Timeline

## Summary
Add a timeline view to the agent session modal showing a chronological visualization of tool calls, messages, and key events. Makes it easier to audit what happened during a long session at a glance.

## Implementation Plan
1. Parse JSONL session files for tool calls and message types
2. Build a `TimelineView` component (vertical list, time-stamped)
3. Add a "Timeline" tab to the existing agent session modal
4. Color-code by event type: user, assistant, tool call

## Estimated Effort
Medium — 3–4 hours.

## Why It Matters
Right now the session modal is a raw chat view. A timeline makes debugging and review significantly faster.

title: Cron Health Heatmap
subtitle: Visual history grid of cron job run results
description:
# Idea: Cron Health Heatmap

## Summary
Add a GitHub-style contribution heatmap to each cron job row showing run history. Green = success, red = error, grey = no run. Gives instant health overview without opening drawers.

## Implementation Plan
1. Extend `/api/crons` route to include run history per job
2. Build a `MiniHeatmap` component (7×N grid, last 28 days)
3. Embed in the cron jobs table row
4. Tooltip on hover showing date + status

## Estimated Effort
Medium — depends on run history API availability.

## Why It Matters
Right now you only see the last run. A heatmap surfaces intermittent failures immediately.
